title: Payments & Wallet — ADA and Stripe class purchase flows
description: Epic E-01. All payment-related features for M4: live ADA/JPY price conversion and display with 60s refresh and 5% drift alert (F-01); ADA purchase via CIP-30 double-sign flow, pessimistic 10-block confirmation, pending state display (F-02); Stripe credit card purchase Japan-compliant with immediate access on success (F-03); parallel payment path presentation with graceful degradation when either path is unavailable (F-04); wallet connection/disconnection, unsupported wallet, wrong network handling — all supported CIP-30 wallets (F-12); admin-initiated ADA and Stripe refunds with class access revocation and reward invalidation flag (F-13); Cardano network status indicator (healthy/degraded/unreachable) (F-14); student purchase history including pending, completed, and refunded transactions (F-15).

pre_conditions: Blockfrost API key (preprod) is configured in .env
web3/config/preprod.json contains current Conway-era protocol parameters (v10.0, epoch >=275)
Stripe API keys (STRIPE_KEY, STRIPE_SECRET) are configured in .env
Stripe account is configured for Japan: currency set to JPY and domestic payment methods enabled (operator setup prerequisite; not verifiable from source code)
ADA/JPY exchange rate setting exists in the settings table (ada-to-jpy key)
Teachers have set class prices in JPY (existing platform feature)
Student enrollment and class access granting are operational (existing platform feature)
post_conditions: F-01: Details.jsx displays the JPY price and a live ADA equivalent computed from the ada-to-jpy rate; the ADA price refreshes automatically every 60 seconds via setInterval(poll, 60_000); when the refreshed ADA price deviates more than 5% from the quoted price (drift > 0.05), the driftWarning state is set, a visible alert 'ADA price has shifted >5% since page load. Confirm before paying.' is shown, and the ADA payment path is disabled until the student re-initiates; when the ADA/JPY rate is unavailable, the ADA payment path is hidden or disabled with an explanation message and the Stripe payment path remains fully functional

F-02: Students complete ADA purchase via CIP-30 double-sign flow (platform signs first, student signs second via signTx(tx, partial=true)); the pending state UI in Details.jsx displays the live confirmation count as X/10 via PurchaseStatusController, which evaluates the threshold via config('services.cardano.required_confirmations', 10); class access is granted by CoursePurchaseService after exactly 10 on-chain confirmations polled by check-tx-confirmations.mjs; duplicate payment attempts for a class the student already owns or has a pending payment for are rejected at the DB layer via unique constraint in database/migrations/2026_02_18_033218_add_composite_unique_to_nft_transactions_table.php
F-03: Students complete the Stripe checkout flow initiated by StripeService; class access is granted immediately upon a successful Stripe charge event with no confirmation wait

F-04: Both ADA and Stripe payment options are rendered in parallel on Details.jsx with independent conditional branches for each path; degradation of either path (ADA unavailable or Stripe unavailable) renders only that path's error state while the other path remains interactive

F-12: CIP-30 wallet connection and disconnection are handled by Web3WalletController; wallets not in the supported list show a clear error message; a wallet on the wrong Cardano network blocks the ADA payment action and shows an explanation; confirmation count for a pending ADA purchase is resumable on page return — PurchaseStatusController serves the current X/10 count on demand, with no background polling job required

F-13: Admin initiates Stripe refunds via AdminRefundController and ADA refunds via AdminRefundController calling build-refund-tx.mjs; ADA refund outputs are returned to the student's original sending address derived from the purchase UTXO (not a custodial address); on refund confirmation the student's class access is revoked and the course_histories record transitions to refunded status; the rewards_invalidated flag is set to true in course_histories; the refund is visible to admin in the payment dashboard (Admin/WalletHistory); KNOWN GAP: student notification via the Notification model (app/Models/Notification.php) is not yet wired in AdminRefundController or RefundController and must be implemented to fully satisfy F-13

F-14: check-network-health.mjs returns one of three statuses (healthy, degraded, unreachable); the ADA payment UI in Details.jsx reflects the current network status and disables or warns on degraded/unreachable

F-15: PurchaseHistoryService surfaces each student's purchase records including: ADA purchases with tx_hash and live X/10 confirmation count for pending entries served by PurchaseStatusController; Stripe purchases with receipt reference; and refunded entries with refunded status — all rendered in PurchaseHistoryTable.jsx
invariants: A student must not gain class access before ADA payment reaches 10 on-chain confirmations (required_confirmations config key) or before Stripe charge succeeds
A student must not submit a duplicate payment for a class they already own or have a pending payment for (enforced at DB layer via unique constraint in the nft_transactions table)

ADA price quote must be invalidated after the window configured by services.cardano.quote_window_minutes (default 5 minutes); CoursePurchaseService rejects submission of an expired quote with the message 'Price quote expired. Please re-initiate checkout.'

For platform-initiated transactions (purchases, refunds), transaction building and Blockfrost submission happen server-side only: build-purchase-tx.mjs / build-refund-tx.mjs build the transaction, the student signs via CIP-30 signTx(tx, partial=true) returning a TransactionWitnessSet, and submit-signed-tx.mjs merges the witness and submits to Blockfrost. Student self-mint (F-18, rewards contract) is a separate flow and is not governed by this contract.

Recipient addresses for purchase outputs (teacher commission, admin fee) use custodial HD derivation via get-custodial-address.mjs, never the student's CIP-30 spending address. ADA refund outputs are exempt: the refund is returned to the student's original sending address from the purchase UTXO, not a custodial address.
No private key material (ROOT_KEY, OWNER_PKH) may appear in any HTTP response produced by controllers in app/Http/Controllers/ or services in app/Services/

acceptance: Student completes end-to-end ADA purchase: connect CIP-30 wallet -> CoursePurchaseService issues ADA price quote -> student signs tx via CIP-30 signTx(tx, partial=true) -> submit-signed-tx.mjs submits to Blockfrost -> check-tx-confirmations.mjs polls until 10 confirmations -> CoursePurchaseService grants class access and course_histories record transitions to confirmed status

Student completes end-to-end Stripe checkout: initiate Stripe session via StripeService -> complete Stripe payment flow -> StripeService receives successful charge event -> class access granted immediately with course_histories record in completed status

Both ADA and Stripe payment options rendered in parallel on Details.jsx; disabling the ADA path (e.g. network unreachable) leaves the Stripe path fully interactive; disabling the Stripe path leaves the ADA path fully interactive

Admin initiates ADA refund: AdminRefundController calls build-refund-tx.mjs -> refund transaction confirmed on-chain -> course_histories record transitions to refunded status with rewards_invalidated flag set to true -> student class access revoked

Admin initiates Stripe refund: AdminRefundController calls StripeService refund -> Stripe charge reversed -> course_histories record transitions to refunded status with rewards_invalidated flag set to true -> student class access revoked

Student purchase history (PurchaseHistoryTable.jsx) displays: ADA purchase entries with tx_hash and X/10 confirmation count for pending entries; Stripe purchase entries with receipt reference; refunded entries with refunded status label

All supported CIP-30 wallets complete the ADA purchase flow; an unsupported wallet triggers a visible error message; a wallet on the wrong Cardano network blocks submission and shows a network mismatch message
When ADA price drift exceeds 5% between quote and re-check, Details.jsx shows the alert 'ADA price has shifted >5% since page load. Confirm before paying.' and disables the ADA payment path

convergence_checks.description: Notification model exists (F-13 known gap — student notification stub)
Composite unique constraint migration exists (F-02 duplicate payment guard)
Refund status and rewards_invalidated flag migration exists (F-13/F-15)
RewardsWarningDialog exists (F-13 rewards invalidation warning on refund)
ADA price refresh interval 60_000ms present in Details.jsx (F-01 60s polling)
ADA price drift threshold 0.05 present in Details.jsx (F-01 drift alert)
ADA drift alert string present in Details.jsx (F-01 user-visible alert)
ADA availability flag present in Details.jsx (F-04 independent ADA path)
Stripe conditional branch present in Details.jsx (F-04 independent Stripe path)
ADA quote expiry config key present in CoursePurchaseService (F-02 quote TTL invariant)
Expired quote rejection message present in CoursePurchaseService (F-02 invariant)
required_confirmations config key present in PurchaseStatusController (F-02 server-side threshold)
Confirmation count checked in check-tx-confirmations.mjs (F-02 Node.js side)
get-custodial-address.mjs referenced in CoursePurchaseService (purchase recipient address invariant)
refunded ENUM value defined in refund status migration (F-13/F-15 status string)
rewards_invalidated column defined in refund status migration (F-13 rewards flag)
network health 'unreachable' status defined in check-network-health.mjs (F-14)
ADA price freshness browser test exists (F-01/F-14)
ADA conversion failure browser test exists (F-01 degraded path)
Stripe unavailable browser test exists (F-04 degraded path)
